title: SCP3412
treatment_protocol_ch1: Pregnancies were classified based on clinical diagnosis of late-onset preeclampsia (diagnosed ≥34 weeks’ gestation according to ISSHP criteria) or uncomplicated.
extract_protocol_ch1: Tissue samples obtained after delivery of neonate and placenta. Tissue biopsies from placentae were washed in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) before being submerged in RNAlater Stabilisation Solution (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Massachusetts, USA) and stored at -80oC. Tissue was disrupted by homogenizing for 3.5 mins at 30 Hz (TissueLyser, QIAGEN) in 600 μl Buffer RLT Plus (RNeasy Plus Mini Kit; QIAGEN, Victoria, Australia). Total RNA was extracted from the supernatant using the RNeasy Plus Mini Kit (QIAGEN) according to the manufacturer’s protocol.,RNA libraries were prepared for sequencing using standard Illumina protocols
data_processing: Trimming of low-quality bases and adapters was performed using fastp (v0.23.2) with the parameters --cut_right, --cut_window_size 4, --cut_mean_quality 20, and --length_required 75.,Trimmed paired-end reads were aligned to the human genome (GRCh38) using STAR (v2.7.10a) with a sex-informed genome annotation from the XYalign package. Using the pre-built genome index, alignment was performed with the following parameters: --outFilterType BySJout, --outFilterMismatchNmax 999, --outSAMtype BAM SortedByCoordinate and --outSAMmapqUnique 60. Read group information was specified with --outSAMattrRGline.,Read alignments were processed to remove PCR duplicates using Picard MarkDuplicates (v2.26.0, with -REMOVE_DUPLICATES=TRUE).,Gene-level quantification was performed using featureCounts (v2.0.3) with the following parameters: -a gencode.v29.annotation.gtf, -s 2, -p, --countReadPairs, and -T 16.,Assembly: GRCh38,Supplementary files format and content: tab-delimited text file includes raw counts for each Sample
contact_department: College of Medicine and Public Health
contact_address: HMRB Building, Loading Bay via Sports Road
